_about: Source risk registry for The Artist Signal Engine. Edit by hand or regenerate with sources_registry.py --seed. Set terms_checked true only after a human confirms terms.

sources.name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art Open Access
sources.purpose: Public-domain artworks and object metadata for The Frame and historical anchors.
sources.signal_provided: the visual and historical record
sources.notes: Use only items flagged public domain. Confirm CC0 status per object.

sources.name: Art Institute of Chicago API
sources.purpose: Open collection metadata and public-domain images.
sources.signal_provided: the visual and historical record
sources.notes: Check is_public_domain per object before reuse.

sources.name: Cleveland Museum of Art Open Access
sources.signal_provided: the visual and historical record
sources.notes: Filter to open-access items.

sources.name: Smithsonian Open Access
sources.purpose: CC0 collection metadata and media across Smithsonian units.
sources.signal_provided: the visual and historical record
sources.notes: Requires an API key. Spans all 19 Smithsonian museums plus research centers, including the National Museum of African American History and Culture, the National Portrait Gallery, and the Archives of American Art. Use CC0 items only.

sources.name: Wikimedia Commons
sources.purpose: Public-domain and openly licensed media for historical anchors.
sources.signal_provided: the deep well of memory
sources.notes: Confirm the exact license per file and attribute as required.

sources.name: OpenAlex
sources.purpose: Open scholarly metadata for the lineage under today's conversations.
sources.signal_provided: the historical and theoretical depth
sources.rate_limit: polite pool, set a contact email
sources.notes: Metadata is open; full texts follow their own licenses.

sources.name: Directory of Open Access Journals
sources.signal_provided: the lineage under today's conversations
sources.notes: Summarize and link; confirm each article's license.

sources.name: arXiv
sources.purpose: Public technology and AI research that reshapes artists' tools and rights.
sources.signal_provided: what is coming for artists' tools and rights
sources.notes: Summarize and link; do not redistribute full PDFs.

sources.name: Open calls and residencies (add specific feeds)
sources.purpose: The opportunity layer and a future paid listing product.
sources.signal_provided: where money, time, and space are being offered to artists
sources.notes: Placeholder. Add specific public listing feeds only after checking robots.txt and terms.

sources.name: National Gallery of Art (NGA Images)
sources.purpose: Open-access public-domain artworks and open collection data.
sources.signal_provided: the visual and historical record
sources.notes: Open-access images via NGA Images; open data on GitHub. Use items marked open access.

sources.name: Getty Open Content
sources.purpose: Over 150,000 public-domain images and open linked-data vocabularies.
sources.signal_provided: the visual and historical record
sources.notes: Use Open Content Program items. Getty also offers IIIF and linked-data vocabularies.

sources.name: Rijksmuseum
sources.purpose: Public-domain masterworks with a long-standing open API.
sources.signal_provided: the visual and historical record
sources.license: public domain / CC0 for public-domain works
sources.notes: Requires a free API key. Use public-domain works only.

sources.name: New York Public Library Digital Collections
sources.purpose: Hundreds of thousands of public-domain images, maps, photographs, and manuscripts.
sources.signal_provided: the deep well of memory
sources.license: public domain for items marked public domain
sources.notes: Requires an access token. Filter to public-domain items.

sources.name: Library of Congress (Free to Use and Reuse)
sources.purpose: Public-domain photography, documents, and themed sets: civil rights, founding documents, diaspora histories.
sources.signal_provided: the documentary record of democracy, rights, and marginalized voices
sources.license: public domain / no known restrictions per item
sources.rate_limit: public API, no key, rate-limit politely
sources.notes: Use the Free to Use sets and items marked public domain or no known restrictions. Not every LoC item is public domain.

sources.name: U.S. National Archives (NARA)
sources.purpose: Federal records in the public domain, including the founding documents: the Constitution, the Bill of Rights, the Declaration.
sources.signal_provided: the founding documents as public-domain material
sources.notes: U.S. government works carry no copyright. The Constitution and Bill of Rights are free to reproduce and remix.

sources.name: Digital Public Library of America
sources.purpose: An aggregator of millions of items from U.S. libraries, archives, and museums.
sources.signal_provided: a wide net across many institutions at once
sources.license: rights vary per item; check each
sources.notes: Aggregated metadata; rights vary per item, so confirm each item before reuse.

sources.name: Wikidata
sources.purpose: Public-domain structured facts: birth and death dates, places, mediums, links. Powers The Almanac.
sources.signal_provided: clean dated facts for the daily date piece
sources.notes: CC0 facts. Free to state; we still write original prose and link out.

sources.name: Wikipedia (anniversaries and context)
sources.purpose: Daily anniversaries and context for The Almanac.
sources.signal_provided: context for the day's anchor
sources.notes: Text is CC BY-SA, so summarize in our own words and cite; never copy.

sources.name: MoMA collection data (GitHub)
sources.purpose: Public-domain metadata for 150,000+ works and artist records, including nationality and gender fields.
sources.signal_provided: facts and a representation lens; NO images
sources.notes: Data only. Images are in copyright and licensed via Art Resource/Scala. Use the facts as material, never the images. A springboard for Rey's own original work.

sources.name: Pérez Art Museum Miami
sources.purpose: Miami and Caribbean and Latinx diaspora exhibitions and news; a portal source, not a data trove.
sources.signal_provided: Miami, Caribbean, and Latinx art news and exhibitions
sources.notes: Contemporary collection, no open-access images. Link and summarize exhibitions and news only. Check terms before any automated fetch.

sources.name: National YoungArts Foundation
sources.purpose: Miami-based national foundation for young artists; open calls, awards, and emerging-artist news.
sources.signal_provided: opportunities and the next generation of artists
sources.notes: Use for open calls, deadlines, winners, and Miami programming. Link and summarize. A natural partner, not a data source.

sources.name: Alldayeveryday (creative production company)
sources.purpose: An LA Arts District production company; a source for the creative-industry and moving-image beat, and a model for a studio arm.
sources.signal_provided: the production and creative-industry beat
sources.notes: Commercial and brand-adjacent. Use as industry news and inspiration, never a content trove. More relevant to a future studio arm than to the daily wire.
